description: The running local service should clearly let the user connect to nearby repositories or workspaces that contain a .bowman directory. The UX should make it obvious what project is open, how projects are discovered or selected, and what Aimparency writes into the local repo.

reflections.context: Make the local .bowman project connection flow intuitive for first-time open source users.
reflections.outcome: Completed three core UX improvements: documented .bowman layout in README, added visible project path in app header, and implemented explicit local project discovery with click-to-open suggestions. One supporting aim (watchdog reconnection) remains partially complete but is polish rather than core functionality.
reflections.effectiveness: Breaking the UX goal into specific, testable sub-aims allowed focused implementation. Each piece improved orientation independently: docs for understanding, header for runtime awareness, discovery for reducing manual path entry.
reflections.lesson: When a parent aim has mostly-complete supporting work, mark the parent done once core functionality is delivered. Don't let polish items block completion signals.
reflections.pattern: This follows the documentation aim pattern: verify supporting work is substantially complete, then mark parent done to keep the aim graph accurate.

status.comment: Core project connection UX is complete: .bowman data layout documented, current project visible in header, local project discovery implemented with explicit picker flow. Watchdog reconnection polish remains partially complete but doesn't block basic project connection.
